data.example: Single HELP registry co-located with the AnalysisSpec registry: {title, algorithm, what_it_shows, docs_url, optional mermaid}. Never hard-code descriptions in templates; the popover summarizes, the docs/ page is source of truth (AGENTS.md rule 4).
data.name: Every analysis/plot ships a ? help popover

data.rationale: Default to only the best wheel dataset per animal+date (config-overridable): manual quality rank first; if unavailable a composite score gated at >=200 cells AND >=10 min, then more-cells > longer-duration, SNR-weighted. Full ranking formula in configs/pipeline.yaml (Plan 06 Task 4.2.1).
data.title: Analyse the best wheel dataset per animal+date by default

data.rationale: io/dataset.py::Session.load_event_traces(prefer='spks', clean=True, version=None) returns deconvolved suite2p spikes when prefer='spks' and present, else the Plan-03 fluor-step array. Every addon seam (Plan 08 _geometry_signal, 09, 10 load_deconvolved, 12 _drift_signal) resolves to THIS accessor.
data.title: Session.load_event_traces is the single canonical event/signal accessor

data.rationale: analysis/registration.py IoU+Hungarian engine (register_sessions/register_sessions_aligned) is the well-tested live tracker. Paul's DTW tracker is dead/incomplete (missing find_overlaps/normalize_dtw_distances/store_*; scorer wrong without normalization; method='weighted' mislabeled). Plan 07 renames method to 'iou_hungarian' (weighted alias) + adds 'dtw' after porting/fixing 4 funcs + ground truth.
data.title: IoU+Hungarian is the live longitudinal tracker; Paul's DTW tracker is dead code

data.rationale: Every analysis result AND dataset array is loaded from the master HDF5 when already present — keyed by dataset + QC version + fluor_version + params via the result_store partition / get_or_compute seam (read_result_partition / write_result_partition) — and recomputed only on a genuine cache miss. Makes the intrinsic pipeline efficient, reproducible and precise. Batch (run_analysis) and web (flow run) paths must share one partition scheme.
data.title: A10: load-if-cached — never recompute a cached result or dataset array

data.rationale: The goal changed (overhaul-user-input-prompts.md): merging target is basic-analysis rather than neural-analysis; all merging should essentially be done, but ensure no important parts are missing while working tasks. The old agent_todo.md and Intrinsic TODO.md are outdated.
data.title: Merge everything into basic-analysis, not neural-analysis

data.rationale: Memory moved out of the shared `memory` graph into a dedicated `basic-analysis` graph declared in agent-skills cluster.yaml, so an overwrite or bad write in another project cannot touch it. .mcp.json pins OMNIGRAPH_GRAPH_ID=basic-analysis; a second `omnigraph-globals` server exposes the shared `memory` graph, because the MCP bridge is pinned to one graph via env var and none of its tools take a graph argument -- reading both project memory and global-scope Preferences therefore needs two servers. Migrated 2026-07-16 with infra/mcp-servers/scripts/split-project-graph.py: 95 nodes / 99 edges copied; source `memory` left intact.
data.title: basic-analysis memory lives in its own Omnigraph graph (per-project isolation)

data.rationale: The repo orchestrates suite2p per subsession, configured via a standard ops file, ported from AnimalClass. Verified current state: io/suite2p.py + preprocessing/io.py only load/parse suite2p outputs — the runner is ABSENT (a verified gap). Plan 04.
data.title: Run suite2p per subsession via a standard ops file (not on a merged mask)

data.rationale: Order raw -> binarize -> scale dF/F -> z-score; every step individually toggle-able and persisted per-step to HDF5; every analysis saved/loaded keyed to the fluor_version it consumed so results are reproducible and comparable. preprocessing/fluor_pipeline.py (Plan 03).
data.title: Versioned 4-step fluorescence pipeline persisted to the master HDF5

data.location: docs/src (Material for MkDocs); mkdocs build --strict is a gate; every analysis has a docs_url page.
data.name: MkDocs documentation

data.rationale: All analysis output consolidated behind one HDF5 layout seam (app/services/h5_paths.resolve_group); pipeline/video/metadata CSVs removed; batch writes go through a staging->merge queue with a reentrant cross-process write lock (basic_analysis/io/hdf5_lock.py) to avoid batch/web write contention.
data.title: Master HDF5 store is the single source of truth (no CSVs)

data.statement: Every analysis and plot must ship a '?' help affordance (help_algorithm/help_shows/docs_url) AND a docs page in the same change; enforced by tests/test_app_help_registry.py and mkdocs --strict. (AGENTS.md rule 4)

data.rationale: 2026-07-08 plan (branch refactor): (1) config translator seam + config page; (2) versioned fluorescence pipeline raw->binarize->dF/F->z-score + suite2p orchestration + store rebuild; (3) video twitch + ROI correction; (4) analysis rerun + significance framework + longitudinal benchmark; (5) addons: population geometry/tangling, topology/Betti, Bayesian+XGBoost decoders, coupling, drift.

data.location: basic_analysis/io/{master_hdf5,video_store,hdf5_lock}.py; app/services/{result_store,store_merge,auto_merge,staging}.py. Reentrant cross-process write lock; staging->merge queue.

data.statement: Report mutual information in bits, not nats (divide KSG/KNN estimates by ln 2) to stay consistent with the binned estimator.
Never delete stat.npy or other raw/derived data files; HDF5 cell-metadata caching is additive only.

data.example: Flag outlier cells/neurons with Tukey's outer fence (Q3 + 3.0*IQR) on per-neuron metrics into outlier_rois.csv; flag, never silently drop.
data.name: Outlier QC via Tukey outer fence

data.example: Use `ruff check <files>` (pyproject ignores E501); passing an explicit --select E,F,I,W overrides the ignore list and reintroduces E501 line-length noise.
data.name: Lint with plain ruff check

data.statement: Every externally-developed method is cited in its docstring, its docs/ page, AND references.md (with a method summary + key insights).
No compute function reads a raw nested YAML path; all config access goes through the translator seam (config/translate.py), which mirrors app/services/h5_paths.py::resolve_group as one mapping table.

Every changed module keeps >=95% line coverage; add tests when defining new functions and rerun tests when a function changes. Command: uv run --extra dev python -m pytest -q.  [+ba-coverage-lint: Aim for >95% test coverage per file; Ruff (rules E,F,I,W, line-length 100) must be clean before done.]

Every analysis is saved AND loaded keyed to the fluorescence-pipeline version (fluor_version) it consumed; analyses run on any fluor version via Session.load_event_traces and metrics not comparable across versions are flagged.
A single global, config-definable random seed is set and honoured everywhere for reproducibility.
No overclaiming: 'putative twitch' (the state gate is an EMG-free surrogate) and drift-without-position uses proxies only with mandatory bleaching controls.

Analysis results and dataset arrays must be load-if-cached from the master HDF5 (keyed by dataset+QC+fluor_version+params via result_store get_or_compute / read_result_partition); recompute only on a genuine cache miss. Batch and web paths must share one partition scheme; a compute-called-once test must guard it.

All heavy compute is parallelized via numba (CPU) or torch (GPU) and ships a benchmark test proving a significant speedup (clustering, geometry, topology + nulls, decoders, twitch, tracking, drift, correlations/STTC).

Results under different QC / dataset-selection / fluor-version go to separate master.h5 partitions; any change to the frame/cell filter creates a new results partition recording the exact frames + cells + results.

Every plot and every test attaches a comparison-to-shuffle null; the test type, its reasoning and n_shuffles appear in the plot legend and are saved to the master HDF5. paired-vs-independent lives in config.
The master HDF5 gives every animal/date/subsession its own isolated space with no spillover — no merged-fluorescence-in-place-of-split-data (the current defect being fixed).

Basic Analysis
data.summary: Flexible pipeline extracting information from neural + behavioral recordings (2P calcium imaging). Research goal: intrinsic activity in hippocampus CA3 in mice, in darkness, no task. Python 3.10.19 + uv. Data on F:\Experiments\Steffen\Intrinsic_Imaging (32 animals, 525 sessions). Importable package basic_analysis/ + Flask web app app/.
